name: Plant Growth and Development
full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Plant Growth and Development

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Plant Growth and Development

questions.question: How many cells per hour does a single maize root apical meristem can give?
questions.question_raw: How many cells per hour does a single maize root apical meristem can give?
questions.explanation: Growth is measured by a variety of parameters some of which are: increase in fresh weight, dry weight, length, area, volume, and cell number. One single maize root apical meristem can give rise to more than 17,500 new cells per hour, whereas cells in watermelon may increase in size by up to 3,50,000 times. In the former, growth is expressed as an increase in cell number; the latter expresses growth as an increase in the size of the cell.

questions.question: The process of de-specialization of differentiated cells so that they become undifferentiated and meristematic is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The process of de-specialization of differentiated cells so that they become undifferentiated and meristematic is called ________.
questions.explanation: The process of de-specialization of differentiated cells so that they become undifferen­tiated and able to divide is known as dedifferentiation. It involves the activation of certain genes that not only reverse differentiation but also stimulate cell division. Cork cambium, wound cambium and inter-fascicular vascular cambium are always produced through dedifferentia­tion.

questions.question: The undifferentiated mass of parenchymatous cells are called:
questions.question_raw: The undifferentiated mass of parenchymatous cells are called:
questions.explanation: Plant callus is a growing mass of unorganized plant parenchyma cells. In living plants, callus cells are those cells that cover a plant wound.

questions.question: The correct sequence leading to the growth curve:
questions.question_raw: The correct sequence leading to the growth curve:
questions.explanation: The initial growth is slow (lag phase), and it increases rapidly thereafter - at an exponential rate (log or exponential phase). Here, both the progeny cells following mitotic cell division retain the ability to divide and continue to do so. However, with limited nutrient supply, the growth slows down leading to a stationary phase.

questions.question: Auxins are generally produced in:
questions.question_raw: Auxins are generally produced in:
questions.explanation: Auxins promote stem elongation, inhibit the growth of lateral buds (maintains apical dominance). They are produced in the stem, buds, and root tips. Example, Indole Acetic Acid (IA). Auxin is a plant hormone produced in the stem tip that promotes cell elongation.

questions.question: The differentiated cells have lost the capacity to divide can regain the capacity of the division under certain conditions called _______.
questions.question_raw: The differentiated cells have lost the capacity to divide can regain the capacity of the division under certain conditions called _______.
questions.explanation: The living differentiated cells that have lost the capacity to divide can regain the capacity of the division under certain conditions. This phenomenon is termed as dedifferentiation.

questions.question: Measurement and the comparison of total growth per unit time is called _________.
questions.question_raw: Measurement and the comparison of total growth per unit time is called _________.
questions.explanation: The absolute growth rate refers to the measurement and comparison of total growth per unit time. Thus, Absolute growth = Growth in a given time period. Relative growth rate refers to the growth of a particular system per unit time, expressed on a common basis.

questions.question: Which of the following events are arranged in the correct order?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following events are arranged in the correct order?
questions.explanation: Growth, differentiation, and development are very closely related events in the life of a plant. Broadly, development is considered as the sum of growth and differentiation.

questions.question: All changes that an organism goes through during its life cycle is called _________.
questions.question_raw: All changes that an organism goes through during its life cycle is called _________.
questions.explanation: Development is a term that includes all changes that an organism goes through during its life cycle from germination of the seed to senescence.

questions.question: The phenomenon of heterophylly is an example of _______.
questions.question_raw: The phenomenon of heterophylly is an example of _______.
questions.explanation: Plants follow different pathways in response to the environment or phases of life to form different kinds of structures. This ability is called plasticity. This phenomenon of heterophylly is an example of plasticity.

questions.question: Auxanometer is used to measure
questions.question_raw: Auxanometer is used to measure
questions.explanation:
An auxanometer (Gr. auxain, "to grow" + metron, "measure") is an apparatus for measuring increase or rate of growth in plants.
In case of an arc-auxanometer (see picture), there is a wire fixed with the plant apex on one end and a dead-weight on the other.

questions.question: Maximum growth occurs in:
questions.question_raw: Maximum growth occurs in:
questions.explanation: In growth curve, maximum growth occurs in the exponential phase of the sigmoid curve. During these periods cells divide mitotically to increase the length and girth of the plant.

questions.question: Which growth is also called apparent growth?
questions.question_raw: Which growth is also called apparent growth?

options.label: A
options.text: Irreversible increase in mass or volume

options.label: B
options.text: Reversible decrease in mass and volume

options.label: C
options.text: Reversible increase in mass and volume

options.label: D
options.text: Irreversible decrease in mass and volume

correct_option_texts: Irreversible increase in mass or volume
questions.explanation: Irreversible increase in mass or volume of plant part is called apparent growth. Apparent growth is permanent and cannot be reversed back as it occurs due to increase in cell number.

questions.question: The S-shaped graph produced on plotting growth against time is called _________.
questions.question_raw: The S-shaped graph produced on plotting growth against time is called _________.
questions.explanation: S-Shaped growth curve (Sigmoid growth curve) A pattern of growth in which, in a new environment, the population density of an organism increases slowly initially, in a positive acceleration phase; then increases rapidly approaching an exponential growth rate, but then declines in a negative acceleration phase until at zero growth rate the population stabilizes. This slowing of the rate of growth reflects increasing environmental resistance which becomes proportionately more important at higher population densities. This type of population growth is termed ‘density-dependent’.

questions.question: The point at which exponential phase ends and decelerating phase begins is called
questions.question_raw: The point at which exponential phase ends and decelerating phase begins is called
questions.explanation:
An inflection point is a point on a curve at which the sign of the curvature (i.e., the concavity) changes. Inflection points may be stationary points, but are not local maxima or local minima.
For example, for the curve plotted given, the red point is an inflection point.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Plant Growth and Development

questions.question: Which of the following areas in the higher plants take part in the formation of new cells?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following areas in the higher plants take part in the formation of new cells?
questions.explanation: The tissues that have the ability of cell division located in a specific area are called meristem. They are located at stem and root tips, nodes of the stem, and intercalary region.

questions.question: Permanent and irreversible increase in dry weight, size, and mass, the volume of cell, organ, or organism is called ________.
questions.question_raw: Permanent and irreversible increase in dry weight, size, and mass, the volume of cell, organ, or organism is called ________.
questions.explanation: Growth is defined as a permanent or irreversible increase in dry weight, size, mass, or volume of a cell, organ, or organism. Growth in living beings is intrinsic or internal.

questions.question: In dicotyledonous plants, the girth of the stem and root increase due to ________.
questions.question_raw: In dicotyledonous plants, the girth of the stem and root increase due to ________.
questions.explanation: Secondary growth is the result of the activity of the two lateral meristems, the cork cambium, and vascular cambium. Arising from lateral meristems, secondary growth increases the girth of the plant root or stem, rather than its length.

questions.question: Mathematically arithmetic growth is expressed as ________.
questions.question_raw: Mathematically arithmetic growth is expressed as ________.
questions.explanation: Arithmetic growth is represented by L_t = L_0 + rt where L_t is final growth, L_0 is the initial size and r_t is the rate of growth and time respectively.

questions.question: It is the testing of biological activity like growth response of a substance by employing a living material like plant or plant part.
questions.question_raw: It is the testing of biological activity like growth response of a substance by employing a living material like plant or plant part.
questions.explanation: Testing of biological activity like the growth response of a substance by employing a living material like a plant or plant part is called bioassay. It is used to know the effect of the substance on plant growth.

questions.question: Exponential growth is dependent on:
questions.question_raw: Exponential growth is dependent on:

options.label: A
options.text: Initial size, nutrient availability and time interval

options.label: B
options.text: Initial size, rate of growth and time interval

options.label: C
options.text: Plant type, initial size and time interval

options.label: D
options.text: Initial size, rate of growth and season

correct_option_texts: Initial size, rate of growth and time interval
questions.explanation: Exponential growth is dependent on initial size, rate of growth, and time interval. L_t = L_0 + rt.

questions.question: Which one is not a phase of Growth?
questions.question_raw: Which one is not a phase of Growth?
questions.explanation: Growth phase consists of leg phase, exponential phase and stable phase. It does not consist of linear phase.

questions.question: The graphical representation of the total growth against time is called _________.
questions.question_raw: The graphical representation of the total growth against time is called _________.
questions.explanation: A growth curve is an empirical model of the evolution of a quantity over time or a graphical representation of how a particular quantity increases over time. Growth curves are used in statistics to determine the type of growth pattern of the quantity - be it linear, exponential or cubic. It is also known as Sigmoid curve.

questions.question: If W_1 is the final size, W_0 is initial size, r is the growth rate and t is a time of growth than Exponential growth is represented by:
questions.question_raw: If W1 is the final size, W0 is initial size, r is the growth rate and t is a time of growth than Exponential growth is represented by:
questions.explanation: Exponential growth is represented by initial size and growth rate and time. If W_1 is the final size, W_0 is the initial size, r is the growth rate and t is the time of growth than exponential growth is W_1 = W_0.e^rt

questions.question: Natural cytokinins are synthesized in:
questions.question_raw: Natural cytokinins are synthesized in:
questions.explanation: Natural cytokinins are synthesised in regions where rapid cell division occurs, for example, root apices.

questions.question: Which one prevents premature fall of fruit?
questions.question_raw: Which one prevents premature fall of fruit?
questions.explanation: They help to prevent fruit and leaf drop at early stages but promote the abscission of older mature leaves and fruits.

questions.question: Which hormone is responsible for seed dormancy?
questions.question_raw: Which hormone is responsible for seed dormancy?
questions.explanation: Abscisic acid encourages fruit to drop and leaves to fall. When the plant is low on water abscisic acid closes the stomata, pores in the leaves. It encourages both seeds and plants to go dormant. The hormone induces seeds to produce proteins to store when the seeds are dormant.

questions.question: Auxin was isolated from tips of coleoptiles of oat seedlings by:
questions.question_raw: Auxin was isolated from tips of coleoptiles of oat seedlings by:
questions.explanation: F. W. Went isolated auxins (1926) from tips of coleoptiles of oat seedlings. Isolation of auxins from tips of coleoptiles of oat seedlings is based upon. Avena coleoptile curvature test. The result will be differential growth and formation of curvature. Degree of curvature is almost proportional to the concentration of auxin.

questions.question: Auxins are mostly produced in:
questions.question_raw: Auxins are mostly produced in:
questions.explanation: Auxins are generally produced in growing apices of shoot and root. These two regions are the meristematic cells that are actively taken part in cell division.

questions.question: Natural auxins among the following are:
questions.question_raw: Natural auxins among the following are:
questions.explanation: Auxins like IAA and indole butyric acid (IBA) have been isolated from plants. (naturally). NAA (naphthalene acetic acid) and 2, 4-D (2, 4-dichlorophenoxyacetic) are synthetic auxins.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Plant Growth and Development

questions.question: Abscisic acid is also known as:
questions.question_raw: Abscisic acid is also known as:
questions.explanation: ABA stimulates the closure of stomata in the epidermis and increases the tolerance of plants to various kinds of stresses. Therefore, it is also called the stress hormone.

questions.question: In rosette plants, bolting is due to:
questions.question_raw: In rosette plants, bolting is due to:
questions.explanation: Gibberellins also promote bolting (internode elongation just prior to flowering) in beet, cabbages, and many plants with rosette habit.

questions.question: Which plant hormone is named anti-ageing hormone?
questions.question_raw: Which plant hormone is named anti-ageing hormone?
questions.explanation: A unique role of cytokinins is to produce anti-ageing effects on some plant parts. Cytokinins actually provide a younger, healthier look in plants. By adding this hormone to cut flowers, florists are able to slow down the ageing process, providing us with prettier flowers for longer.

questions.question: In higher plants, the growing apical bud inhibits the growth of the lateral buds. This phenomenon is called _______.
questions.question_raw: In higher plants, the growing apical bud inhibits the growth of the lateral buds. This phenomenon is called _______.
questions.explanation: Apical dominance is the phenomenon whereby the main, central stem of the plant is dominant over (i.e., grows more strongly than) other side stems; on a branch, the main stem of the branch is further dominant over its own side branchlets.

questions.question: Growth regulator ethylene is used for:
questions.question_raw: Growth regulator ethylene is used for:

options.label: C
options.text: Speeding up of ripening of fruits

options.label: D
options.text: Slowing down the ripening of fruits

correct_option_texts: Speeding up of ripening of fruits
questions.explanation: Ethylene is highly effective in fruit ripening. It enhances the respiration rate during the ripening of the fruits.

questions.question: Which of the following gibberellic acid is used for malting process in the brewing industry?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following gibberellic acid is used for malting process in the brewing industry?
questions.explanation: GA_3 is used to speed up the malting process in the brewing industry.

questions.question: Which of the following hormone is used to kill dicotyledonous weeds?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following hormone is used to kill dicotyledonous weeds?
questions.explanation: 2, 4 D (2,4-Dichlorophenoxyacetic acid) are widely used as herbicides. It is widely used to kill dicotyledonous weeds, does not affect mature monocotyledonous plants.

questions.question: Parthenocarpy can be induced to develop seedless fruit by application of:
questions.question_raw: Parthenocarpy can be induced to develop seedless fruit by application of:
questions.explanation: Auxins (IAA, IBA) also induce parthenocarpy, (Seed fewer fruits) e.g., in tomatoes.

questions.question: Identify the cytokinin given below.
questions.question_raw: Identify the cytokinin given below.
questions.explanation: Cytokinin is of different types. The cytokinin shown above is of kinetin which promotes cell division. It was isolated by Miller and Skoog from autoclaved herring sperm DNA.

questions.question: Plant growth regulator that is used in delay in leaf senescence is_______.
questions.question_raw: Plant growth regulator that is used in delay in leaf senescence is_______.
questions.explanation: Cytokinins help overcome apical dominance. They promote nutrient mobilization which helps in the delay of leaf senescence.

questions.question: Kinetin was identified by:
questions.question_raw: Kinetin was identified by:
questions.explanation: The discovery of Kinetin is comparatively more recent. Its credit goes to Miller et al (1955) who were working in Prof. Skoog’s laboratory at the University of Wisconsin on the growth of tobacco pith callus in culture and wanted it to grow indefinitely.

questions.question: A farmer grows cucumber plants in his field. He wants to increase the number of female flowers in them. Which plant hormones can be applied to achieve this?
questions.question_raw: A farmer grows cucumber plants in his field. He wants to increase the number of female flowers in them. Which plant hormones can be applied to achieve this?
questions.explanation: Ethylene promotes female flowers in cucumbers thereby increasing the yield.

questions.question: Ethylene is a:
questions.question_raw: Ethylene is a:
questions.explanation: Ethylene is a simple gaseous hormone. It is synthesized in large amounts by tissues undergoing senescence and ripening fruits.

questions.question: The most commonly used compound as a source of ethylene
questions.question_raw: The most commonly used compound as a source of ethylene
questions.explanation: The most widely used compound as a source of ethylene is ethephon. Ethephon in an aqueous solution is readily absorbed and transported within the plant and releases ethylene slowly.

questions.question: How many numbers of gibberellins are distributed in different organisms?
questions.question_raw: How many numbers of gibberellins are distributed in different organisms?
questions.explanation: There are more than 100 gibberellins reported from widely different organisms such as fungi and higher plants. They are denoted as GA1, GA2, GA3, and so on.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Plant Growth and Development

questions.question: Phytochrome is sensitive to:
questions.question_raw: Phytochrome is sensitive to:
questions.explanation: Phytochrome is a photoreceptor and a pigment that plants, and some bacteria and fungi, use to detect light. It is sensitive to light in the red and far-red regions of the visible spectrum.

questions.question: Antiauxin used in picking cotton bolls is______.
questions.question_raw: Antiauxin used in picking cotton bolls is______.
questions.explanation: TIBA (2,3,5 Triidobenzoic acid) acts as antiauxin that inhibits root and shoots formation. TIBA is used as an antiauxin in picking cotton bolls.

questions.question: Which pair of hormones promotes femaleness in flowers?
questions.question_raw: Which pair of hormones promotes femaleness in flowers?
questions.explanation:
Flower Sex Conversions:
 Gibberellins (GA_3) promote maleness
 Ethylene and auxins promote femaleness

questions.question: What would be expected to happen if a rotten fruit gets mixed with unripe fruits?
questions.question_raw: What would be expected to happen if a rotten fruit gets mixed with unripe fruits?

options.label: C
options.text: Slow down the ripening of unripe fruits

options.label: D
options.text: Increasing to ripen of unripe fruits

correct_option_texts: Increasing to ripen of unripe fruits
questions.explanation: Rotten fruit produces ethylene gas that hastens the ripening of unripe fruits. So, if a rotten fruit gets mixed with unripe fruits the remaining unripe get ripen faster.

questions.question: If GA3 is applied to the rice seedlings, the plant will show:
questions.question_raw: If GA3 is applied to the rice seedlings, the plant will show:
questions.explanation: GA3 is gibberellin that helps in the elongation of the stem of the plant. If the GA3 plant hormone is applied to the rice seedling the plant will show extra elongation of the plant body.

questions.question: You forget to add Cytokinin the culture medium, there will be very little growth as Cytokinin is essential for:
questions.question_raw: You forget to add Cytokinin the culture medium, there will be very little growth as Cytokinin is essential for:
questions.explanation: Cytokinin is added to the culture medium to induce cell division and cell differentiation. If cytokine is not added to the culture medium, there will be very little growth in culture.

questions.question: A student culture a callus from the tobacco pith in sterilized minimal nutritive medium but add more Cytokinin than Auxin. What would develop first from callus?
questions.question_raw: A student culture a callus from the tobacco pith in sterilized minimal nutritive medium but add more Cytokinin than Auxin. What would develop first from callus?
questions.explanation: Shoots

questions.question: In the wheat field, some broad-leaved weeds were seen by a farmer, which plant hormone would you suggest getting rid of the same?
questions.question_raw: In the wheat field, some broad-leaved weeds were seen by a farmer, which plant hormone would you suggest getting rid of the same?
questions.explanation: Plant hormone 2 : 4-D and 2 : 4 : 5 T have ability to kills the weeds in the crop field. Weeds are unwanted plants that grow along with crop plants and compete with the crop for nutrient and water.

questions.question: Auxin precursor for IAA is:
questions.question_raw: Auxin precursor for IAA is:
questions.explanation: The major precursor of Auxin is most likely the amino acid Tryptophan. However, the tryptophan is possibly derived from the shikimic acid pathway. Numerous enzymes would be utilized for Auxin alone. the ultimate precursor depends on how far you want to follow the production back.

questions.question: Photoperiodism stimulus is perceived by
questions.question_raw: Photoperiodism stimulus is perceived by
questions.explanation: The response of plants to periods of day/night is termed photoperiodism. The site of perception of light/dark duration is the leaves.

questions.question: Flowering in short-day plants is stimulated by:
questions.question_raw: Flowering in short-day plants is stimulated by:

options.label: C
options.text: Short day and uninterrupted long nights

options.label: D
options.text: Short day and interrupted long nights

correct_option_texts: Short day and uninterrupted long nights
questions.explanation: Short-day (i.e.long-night) plants flower as days grow shorter (and nights grow longer) after 21 June in the northern hemisphere, which is during summer or fall. Short-day plants flower when the night lengths exceed their critical photoperiod. They cannot flower under short nights or if a pulse of artificial light is shone on the plant for several minutes during the night; they require a continuous period of darkness before floral development can begin.

questions.question: Vernalisation takes place in response to:
questions.question_raw: Vernalisation takes place in response to:
questions.explanation: There are plants for which flowering is either quantitatively or qualitatively dependent on exposure to low temperature. This phenomenon is termed vernalization.

questions.question: A process of shortening of the juvenile or vegetative phase and hastening flowering by a previous cold treatment is
questions.question_raw: A process of shortening of the juvenile or vegetative phase and hastening flowering by a previous cold treatment is
questions.explanation: Vernalization is the induction of a plant's flowering process by exposure to the prolonged cold of winter, or by an artificial equivalent.

questions.question: Short day plants are also called long night plant because they require a continuous:
questions.question_raw: Short day plants are also called long night plant because they require a continuous:
questions.explanation: A plant that requires a long period of darkness, is termed a "short day" (long night) plant. Short-day plants form flowers only when day length is less than about 12 hours. Many spring and fall flowering plants are short-day plants.

questions.question: Photoperiodism was discovered by:
questions.question_raw: Photoperiodism was discovered by:
questions.explanation: In 1920, W. W. Garner and H. A. Allard published their discoveries on photoperiodism and felt it was the length of daylight that was critical, but it was later discovered that the length of the night was the controlling factor.
